antipattern.symptom: ImportError: cannot import name 'User' OR `User` resolves to the wrong app's model. Adding a second app with `User` silently shadows the original.
antipattern.root-cause: Cross-app import without alias: `from apps.users.models import User`. The module-level name `User` is bound to whatever was imported last; two apps with the same name collide.
antipattern.fix: `from apps.users import models as user_models; user_models.User`. Alias convention is mandatory by rule r4-alias-convention. Detector: grep `from apps\.[a-z_]+\.(models|services) import [A-Z]`.

antipattern.symptom: Code review takes 3x longer because reviewers can't tell where a name came from. mypy reports "Name 'foo' is not defined" intermittently.
antipattern.root-cause: `from apps.foo.models import *` pollutes the importing module with every public name from the source. Private names with no leading underscore leak in too.
antipattern.fix: Replace wildcard with explicit imports. policies.wildcard_allowed=false is forbidden. Detector: grep `^from .+ import \*`.

antipattern.symptom: DRF serializer raises TypeError at runtime: "ForwardRef cannot be evaluated." drf-spectacular fails to produce OpenAPI schema.
antipattern.root-cause: `from __future__ import annotations` was added at the top of a serializer file. DRF's get_type_hints() and drf-spectacular's schema generator cannot resolve the stringified ForwardRefs at the time they need real types.
antipattern.fix: Do NOT add `from __future__ import annotations` to apps/*/serializers.py. The repo ruff config adds a per-file ignore that detects + forbids this in serializer files.

antipattern.symptom: SyntaxError at module import: "lazy import syntax not supported." Build fails on Python 3.12/3.13.
antipattern.root-cause: PEP 810 `lazy from heavy_module import HeavyClass` was used on Python < 3.14, where the syntax does not exist.
antipattern.fix: Use a function-scoped import or `importlib.import_module()` until Python 3.14 is the project minimum. Spec policies.python_version pins the floor; lint rule blocks `^lazy from `.

antipattern.symptom: Moving `apps/users/` to `apps/accounts/users/` breaks every `from ...users.models import` in a different app. CI fails on every dependent import.
antipattern.root-cause: Multi-dot relative imports (`from ..` / `from ...`) couple modules to the physical directory layout. Any restructuring breaks them.
antipattern.fix: Replace with absolute cross-app imports: `from apps.users import models as user_models`. policies.multi_dot_relative_allowed=false is forbidden by the spec.
